assertions.expectation: Identifies Service A (minAvailable=100%) as a guaranteed upgrade blocker — no disruptions allowed at all
assertions.reason: The model correctly identifies Service A as risky because it will 'block the node drain immediately' and mathematically forbid evicting even a single pod.

assertions.expectation: Identifies Service B (maxUnavailable=0) as also blocking upgrades — same effect as 100% minAvailable
assertions.reason: The model correctly identifies Service B as risky for the same reasons as Service A, noting it will block the node drain immediately.

assertions.expectation: States Service C (minAvailable=2 with 3 replicas) is safe — allows 1 pod disruption at a time
assertions.reason: The model states Service C is safe because it 'Allows 1 pod to be evicted at a time'.

assertions.expectation: States Service D (maxUnavailable=1 with 5 replicas) is safe — allows 1 pod down at a time
assertions.reason: The model states Service D is safe because it 'Allows 1 pod to be evicted at a time'.

assertions.expectation: Recommends fixing Service A: change to minAvailable=N-1 or maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: The model recommends changing Service A to allow at least 1 pod to be disrupted and suggests patching it to `maxUnavailable: 1`.

assertions.expectation: Recommends fixing Service B: change to maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: The model recommends changing Service B to `maxUnavailable: 1`.

assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE respects PDBs for up to 1 hour during surge upgrades, then may force-drain
assertions.reason: The model explicitly states that GKE only respects a PDB for up to 1 hour per node, after which it will ignore the PDB and force-delete the pod.

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl patch commands to fix the problematic PDBs
assertions.reason: The model provides exact `kubectl patch pdb` commands for both Service A and Service B.

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing PDB changes in a non-production environment first
assertions.reason: The model does not mention testing the PDB changes in a non-production or staging environment before applying them.
